LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN MULTI-CINEMA, INC. AND AMC ENTERTAINMENT, INC'S MOTION TO DETERMINE/CONFIRM GOOD FAITH OF SETTLEMENT - THE MOTION FOR GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. THE MOVING PARTY PRESENTS SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS IN THE BALLPARK WITHIN THE MEANING OF TECH-BILT, INC. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOC., (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488. THE OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO MEET ITS STATUTORY BURDEN TO PRESENT EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THAT THE SETTLEMENT LACKED GOOD FAITH. SEE CCP 877.6(D). (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. This dismissal pertains to the remaining cross complaint(s). JUDGE CYNTHIA M. LEE; Clerk KCox; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Off Calendar Jan-17-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-06-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn To Determine/Confirm Good Faith Of Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT CARTWRIGHT, TEL. NO. 433-0444 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS AMERICAN MULTI-CINEMA, INC. AND AMC ENTERTAINMENT, INC'S Motion To Determine/Confirm Good Faith Of Settlement - The motion for good faith settlement is granted. The moving party presents substantial evidence showing that the settlement is in the ballpark within the meaning of Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. The opposing party failed to meet its statutory burden to present evidence demonstrating that the settlement lacked good faith. See CCP 877.6(d). (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
